Hero.headline: Building scalable data-driven web solutions
Hero.subheadline: I design and build end-to-end software systems — from data pipelines and backend architecture to modern, maintainable web interfaces.
Hero.paragraph: I focus on clean architecture, clear communication, and long-term maintainability, helping teams deliver solutions that create real business value.
Hero.availability: Currently available for remote freelance projects
Hero.footer: Based in Munich · Working remotely with international clients

Services.title: Services
Services.subtitle: Full lifecycle software engineering focused on robust architecture, data integrity, and long-term maintainability.

data.title: Data Engineering
data.description: Reliable data systems built for traceability, consistency, and analytical readiness.
bullets: Structured cloud-based ETL/ELT pipelines and data modeling
Dashboards and structured datasets designed for reliable analysis

backend.title: Backend Engineering
backend.description: Backend systems designed for scalability, controlled access, and production reliability.
bullets: Secure API design and service integration
Transactional data modeling and system consistency
Debugging, performance tuning, and operational stability

fullstack.title: Full-Stack Development
fullstack.description: Production-ready web applications built with clean architecture and system awareness.
bullets: Clear backend–frontend separation and feature delivery
Responsive interfaces with maintainable code structure

TechStack.title: Technologies & Platforms
TechStack.subtitle: These are the main tools and technologies I use in projects. I adapt to existing architectures and contribute to defining pragmatic, production-ready stacks.

Projects.title: Selected Projects
Projects.subtitle: A selection of systems and applications I’ve designed and implemented — spanning cloud data pipelines, backend integrations, and full-stack systems.

telemetryPipeline.name: Cloud-Native Automotive Telemetry Data Pipeline
telemetryPipeline.details: Azure-native telemetry pipeline designed to ingest high-frequency vehicle signals, transform raw logs with KQL, and supports fast, structured analysis in ADX.
highlights: ADX ingestion and KQL transformations
Analytics-ready datasets powering engineering dashboards
Pragmatic design for reliability and scale

scimApi.name: User Provisioning Integration API
scimApi.details: SCIM-compatible integration API designed to process provisioning events and reliably synchronize internal roles and teams across systems.
highlights: SCIM event processing and attribute mapping
Transaction-safe persistence and identity state consistency
Cross-system role synchronization via Azure AD

ecommerce.name: Full-Stack E-commerce Platform
ecommerce.details: E-commerce web application featuring authentication, inventory management, and transactional purchase flows built on a layered backend architecture.
highlights: Secure authentication and role-based access with JWT
Consistent transactional handling of products and inventory
Clear backend–frontend separation and API-driven integration

speechTranslation.name: Real-Time Multilingual Speech Translation System
speechTranslation.details: Real-time speech processing pipeline that captures live audio, performs transcription and multilingual translation, and streams browser-based captions.
highlights: Real-time audio ingestion and speech-to-text processing
Multilingual translation with Azure integration
Caption streaming to browser-based display UI

reliabilityModel.name: Probabilistic Reliability Modeling for Water Treatment
reliabilityModel.details: Probabilistic model for multi-stage water treatment systems, leveraging Bayesian networks to estimate regulatory compliance under uncertainty.
highlights: Bayesian reliability modeling with scenario-based reliability analysis
Quantification of uncertainty in regulatory compliance estimates
Structured, reproducible model configuration and statistical outputs

trainingLog.name: Training Log Management System
trainingLog.details: Database-backed training management tool designed for structured workout tracking, progress analysis, and trend visualization over time.
highlights: Relational data modeling for workouts, sets, and historical tracking
Analytical queries and visualization of volume progression
Lightweight, local-first architecture with portable deployment

gameTracker.name: Game Inventory & Progress Tracker
gameTracker.details: Desktop application for structured management of game inventories and progression states, backed by a relational SQLite database.
highlights: Relational data modeling for inventory and status management
Local persistence and query handling with SQLite
Lightweight desktop architecture for structured data management

Life.title: Beyond Work
Life.subtitle: Work matters — but so does everything that happens outside of it. These are some of the things that keep me grounded, motivated and curious in everyday life.

psychology.title: Psychology & Human Behaviour
psychology.p1: I’m interested in how people think, make decisions, and collaborate.
psychology.p2: Reading about psychology and communication helps me work effectively across technical and non-technical contexts.

movement.title: Movement & Training
movement.p1: I’m a long-term sports enthusiast. I enjoy CrossFit, strength training, functional workouts, yoga, and swimming.
movement.p2: Training helps me stay healthy, build resilience, and keep enough energy for daily life.

travel.title: Travel & Culture
travel.p1: I love traveling, discovering new places, and learning about different cultures, languages, and ways of life.
travel.p2: Each trip gives me perspective, creativity, and a deeper appreciation for how diverse and interconnected the world is.

games.title: Interactive Worlds & Storytelling
games.p1: I’m drawn to interactive systems, progression mechanics, and long-form storytelling — from complex game worlds to serialized narratives.
games.p2: I enjoy analyzing how rules, structure, and character development shape immersive experiences.

cooking.title: Cooking & Craft
cooking.p1: I enjoy cooking as a structured creative process — experimenting with ingredients, balancing flavors, and refining techniques over time.
cooking.p2: I enjoy spending time trying new recipes and exploring flavors from different cultures.

Contact.title: Let’s work together
Contact.subtitle: If my experience aligns with your project, feel free to reach out. I’m happy to discuss ideas, clarify requirements, and explore potential collaboration.

direct.title: Direct contact
direct.note: I’m currently open to remote and part-time collaborations. A short message describing what you’re building and what you need help with is perfect.

message.title: Send a message
message.body: The fastest way to get started is a short email. If you include the basics below, I can reply with concrete next steps.
bullets: A brief description of your project
message.small: Prefer LinkedIn? That works too — I reply there as well.

email.subject: Project Collaboration — Inquiry
email.body:
Hi Juan,

I came across your work and am interested in discussing a potential collaboration.

Here’s a bit about what I’m working on:

- Project overview:
- Timeline:
- Tech stack (if relevant):

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
